description: A small, dark damselfly in the family Platycnemididae. The species has dark coloration distinguishing it from the related *Arabicnemis caerulea*. It is one of the least-known odonates of the Arabian Peninsula.
behaviour: Found along rocky streams in arid mountain terrain. Little is known about its behaviour due to its remote range.
distribution: *Arabineura khalidi* is known from Oman and Yemen, restricted to the mountainous interior of the Arabian Peninsula.
habitat: Rocky, fast-flowing mountain streams in arid terrain.
sources: Atlas of the European Dragonflies and Damselflies, Jean-Pierre Boudot(Editor), Vincent J Kalkman(Editor), Fons Peels(Illustrator)
Field guide to the dragonflies of Britain and Europe, Klaas-Douwe B Dijkstra.
